name: BD Pyxis MedStation API (BD/CareFusion)
what_it_does: BD Pyxis MedStation REST API for automated medication dispensing cabinet management in hospital settings. Enables AI agents to manage medication cabinet inventory and refill alerts for hospital pharmacy operations, handle controlled substance discrepancy detection and witness workflow for DEA compliance, access dispensing transaction records for medication reconciliation and audit, retrieve pocket and station configuration data for cabinet management, manage formulary updates and medication loading workflow for pharmacy staff operations, handle override and emergency access monitoring for clinical safety oversight, access medication usage analytics and waste tracking for controlled substance management, retrieve machine health and connectivity status for IT operations, manage interface integration with pharmacy information systems and EHR, and access ADC-level analytics for pharmacy KPI reporting.
use_cases: Managing ADC inventory from hospital pharmacy automation agents
Tracking controlled substance discrepancies from DEA compliance agents
Monitoring dispensing analytics from pharmacy management agents
Alerting refill needs from inventory management agents
not_for: Retail pharmacy without hospital/institutional dispensing focus
Ambulatory pharmacy without ADC hardware deployment
Non-medication inventory without DEA Schedule management requirements
best_when: A hospital or health system using BD Pyxis MedStation wants AI agents to automate ADC inventory management, controlled substance tracking, dispensing analytics, and pharmacy operations.
avoid_when: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: BD Pyxis automated dispensing system is a safety-critical medical device — automated overrides of clinical safeguards create patient harm risk. DEA Schedule II-V compliance requires perpetual inventory maintained with every transaction; automated dispense without complete audit trail creates DEA regulatory violation. Joint Commission requires controlled substance diversion investigations; automated discrepancy resolution without pharmacist review creates compliance gaps. BD (Becton Dickinson) acquired CareFusion (Pyxis parent) in 2015 for $12B; API and product roadmap reflects BD medical device company structure.

auth.notes: BD Pyxis uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. API access via BD developer program. No native webhooks. REST API with JSON and HL7. Franklin Lakes, New Jersey HQ (Becton Dickinson). Public (BDX NYSE). BD acquired CareFusion (Pyxis parent) in 2015 ($12B). Pyxis is the market leader in automated dispensing cabinets — 85%+ US hospital market share. Competes with Omnicell for hospital medication automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription plus hardware
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual software subscription; hardware capital or lease
pricing.notes: Franklin Lakes, New Jersey. Becton Dickinson (BDX NYSE). CareFusion acquisition 2015 ($12B). Pyxis 85%+ US hospital ADC market share. Competes with Omnicell for hospital pharmacy automation. 3,400+ hospital customers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per partner integration; documented per deployment

known_agent_gotchas: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Pyxis is FDA-cleared medical device; automated software-level safety override defeats cleared device function and creates regulatory and patient harm liability

DEA Schedule II perpetual inventory — automated controlled substance dispense must maintain 21 CFR Part 1304 biennial inventory accuracy; automated transactions without complete DEA audit trail creates federal violation
Joint Commission diversion investigation — CS discrepancy automated resolution without licensed pharmacist review creates Joint Commission Medication Management standard violation
HIPAA PHI — Pyxis dispensing records contain patient identity + medication data (PHI); BAA required for all integrations; automated data sharing requires HIPAA-compliant data handling
Medical device FDA compliance — Pyxis is FDA 510(k)-cleared medical device; software integration must not modify cleared device software functions or create prohibited device modifications
No webhooks — Pyxis lacks native event webhooks; near-real-time monitoring requires scheduled polling of dispensing transactions and inventory levels
agent_readiness.security_notes: Hospital pharmacy ADC. HIPAA, SOC2, DEA, FDA-510k. OAuth2/API key. US. Medication dispensing and controlled substance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Hospital pharmacy ADC. HIPAA, SOC2, DEA. OAuth2. US. Medication and patient PHI.
